title: ISO/IEC 27035 incident management lifecycle alignment
description: ISO/IEC 27035-1:2023 provides an incident management PROCESS LIFECYCLE and vocabulary, NOT a closed taxonomy of breach types. Used here as an interoperability anchor so a controller's incident-management process feeds the EDPB notification without re-mapping.
source: ISO/IEC 27035-1:2023, Information security incident management - Part 1: Principles and process
caveat: Full normative text is paywalled; phases below are from official ISO abstract/scope and are accurate at lifecycle level. Cite by clause number, do not reproduce paywalled text.

lifecycle.label: Plan and Prepare
lifecycle.desc: Incident management policy, Incident Response Team (IRT), communication & escalation protocols meeting legal/contractual requirements.

lifecycle.label: Detect
lifecycle.desc: Collect situational-awareness info; detect and alert on anomalous/suspicious/malicious activity.

lifecycle.label: Assess
lifecycle.desc: Evaluate and decide (event vs incident; severity).

lifecycle.label: Learn Lessons
lifecycle.desc: Identify and implement improvements; IRT evaluation.

edpb_alignment.note: The EDPB taxonomy (field 60, 25 incident types) and C-I-A nature (field 55) anchor to ISO 27035 terminology/lifecycle so security operations feed the notification. This is an interoperability argument, not a 1:1 type map.
edpb_alignment.edpb_assess_phase: EDPB notification corresponds to ISO 27035 phase 4 (Assess) -> phase 3 (Report) externally to the supervisory authority.
